stories.title: The Underground Library
stories.text: In 1960, a group of children in a small town were told they couldn't use the public library. Instead of giving up, they started their own in an abandoned basement. They collected donated books and took turns being librarian. Within a year, their collection had grown to over 500 books.

stories.title: Desert vs. Tundra
stories.text: Deserts and tundras are both extreme environments, but they differ in important ways. Deserts are scorching hot during the day, while tundras remain frozen year-round. However, both have one thing in common: very little rainfall. Animals in both places have adapted in remarkable ways to survive harsh conditions.
stories.image_prompt: A split scene showing a hot desert with cacti and a lizard on the left, and a frozen tundra with snow and an arctic fox on the right. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters, no names, no labels.

questions.prompt: What do deserts and tundras have in common?

stories.title: The Invention That Changed Breakfast
stories.text: Before the toaster was invented in 1893, people held bread over an open flame with a fork. It was slow, dangerous, and often resulted in burnt toast. Albert Marsh created the first electric toaster, and breakfast was never the same. Today, over 75 million American homes have one.

stories.title: The Letter
stories.text: Amara stared at the letter for a long time. Her grandmother had written it years ago but Amara had never opened it. Her hands trembled as she unfolded the paper. "Dear Amara, if you're reading this, you're brave enough to face anything. Love always, Grandma." Amara smiled through her tears.
